description: The practice of standing together across differences in pursuit of collective liberation. Solidarity encompasses recognizing shared struggles, offering support without saviorism, the understanding that none are free until all are free, and building power through unity.
rationale: The word 'thenu' combines the contemplative 'the' with the grounding 'nu'. This pairing suggests thoughtful rootedness - solidarity not as mere sentiment but as considered commitment to stand firm together. The fricative opening adds gravity to the pledge.
